text: When you start working with APIs, you typically send HTTP requests to a server to get or send data. The server often replies with information in JSON format, which is easy for programs to read. Authentication is important to prove who you are, usually with an API key. You should also be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make within a certain time. Finally, learn to handle errors properly; for example, a 404 code means the resource was not found, while 500 shows a server problem. Good error handling makes your application more reliable.
